title: Prefer message passing to shared mutable memory
description: Move state into a single owning task that receives messages, so concurrency reduces to a serialized mailbox.
body: Use when shared-memory locking is becoming a tangle of locks and invariants. Do not share a struct across tasks with fine-grained locks when a single owner could serialize access. Actions: give the state to one task, have other tasks send requests over a channel/queue, and let the owner process them one at a time; return results via reply channels. Validation signals include no lock around the state, a race detector clean run, and ordering guarantees coming from the single consumer. Risk boundary: unbounded queues can hide backpressure and exhaust memory; bound the channel and decide a drop/block policy, escalating if the policy changes semantics.
